title: 3-Tier Can Organizer
summary: A gravity-feed pantry can rack — three angled 2x4 rails mounted to a 1x12 backer let cans roll forward as you use the front one, sized to any length pantry shelf.
description:
This is a genuinely simple, size-to-fit build: a 1x12 backer board with three 2x4 rails mounted across its face at three different heights, angled slightly so cans naturally roll forward as the front can is used. **Build it to whatever length fits the intended pantry space** — the design is a repeating rail pattern, not a fixed-size case, so measure the shelf first and cut both the backer and rails to match.

Mount the three 2x4 rails with a slight forward tilt built into their spacing, so gravity does the work of keeping cans stocked at the front rather than needing to be pushed forward by hand. Space the rails vertically to match the can heights actually being stored — a rack built for soup cans will need different spacing than one for larger vegetable cans.

This rack can sit flat on an existing shelf or be screwed to the wall of a pantry, depending on the available space.

materials.name: 2x4, three times the length of the 1x12
Brad nails, 2", or self-tapping wood screws

cutList.note: Cut to the length of your own pantry shelf — 24" shown as an example.

cutList.material: 2x4, three times the length of the 1x12
cutList.note: Cut to match the backer board's length.

steps.title: Measure your shelf
steps.body: Measure the pantry shelf where the organizer will sit and set the backer board and rail length to match.

steps.title: Cut the parts
steps.body: Cut the backer board and three rails to the measured length.
materials: 2x4, three times the length of the 1x12

steps.title: Attach the rails
steps.body: Attach the three rails to the backer board at heights matching the cans being stored, with a slight forward tilt.
materials: Brad nails, 2", or self-tapping wood screws
